title: tanacetum parthenium
intro: Tanacetum parthenium, also known as Feverfew, is a perennial flowering plant in the sunflower family native to Europe. The plant has been used medicinally for its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antipyretic properties, which help to relieve fever, headaches, and other symptoms associated with colds and flu. The health benefits of Feverfew include reducing the frequency and severity of migraines, alleviating menstrual cramps and other symptoms associated with PMS, and lowering cholesterol levels. Feverfew can be prepared as a tea, capsule, or tincture, and is also used in herbal remedies such as infusions, salves, and ointments to support overall well-being.
